title: Basic SPL Token Escrow
summary: Implement a two-party SPL token escrow with PDA-backed custody, exchange, and cancel flows.
businessLogic: A maker initializes an escrow by depositing offered tokens into a PDA-controlled vault.
A taker can atomically exchange the requested asset to settle the escrow.
The maker can cancel an unsettled escrow and reclaim the locked asset.

instructions.name: initialize
instructions.description: Create escrow state, derive the vault authority PDA, and move maker funds into the vault.

instructions.name: exchange
instructions.description: Move the taker's requested asset to the maker and release the maker's offered asset from the vault.

instructions.name: cancel
instructions.description: Return the maker's locked asset from the vault when the escrow has not been exchanged.

invariants: Only the stored maker can cancel an escrow.
Vault custody must be controlled by a program-derived authority.
Exchange must validate the expected token mints and payout accounts before moving funds.
Successful settlement or cancellation must close the escrow state and vault account.
failureConditions: Missing maker binding or signer validation.
Missing token mint validation for exchanged assets.
Missing PDA validation for vault authority or escrow derivation.
Allowing payout redirection to attacker-controlled token accounts.
